title: Homer Scissorhands

jokes.text: D'oh! (Homer's signature exclamation)
jokes.punchline: His iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - high character integration and quotability, though familiar

jokes.text: Lisa, this is going too slow for me. And I work at the DMV.
jokes.setup: Character complaining about slow pace of Lisa's painting
jokes.punchline: DMV worker saying something is too slow
jokes.explanation: Solid ironic humor - DMV being notoriously slow makes this character saying something else is slow inherently funny

jokes.text: I'll have to make it more neo-classical and less Mannerist, but okay.
jokes.setup: Lisa responding to request to speed up her painting
jokes.punchline: 8-year-old casually using advanced art terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic Lisa character comedy - her precocious intellectualism contrasted with childish context

jokes.text: I jumped in front of your picture. Now it's ruined.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining what he did to Lisa's painting
jokes.punchline: Bart thinking he can ruin a painting by jumping in front of it like a photo
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic from Bart - confusing photography with painting shows his childish misunderstanding

jokes.text: I'm really sorry it wasn't a year.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying Bart ruined six months of work
jokes.punchline: Bart sarcastically apologizing it wasn't longer
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback with perfect comedic timing - Bart's unapologetic meanness delivered with mock sympathy

jokes.text: I already do. It's too close to Christmas.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying Bart will regret the day he was born
jokes.punchline: Bart's practical complaint about his birthday timing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - expected self-pity becomes relatable practical complaint about birthday/Christmas gift consolidation

jokes.text: Oh, I get to enjoy this, but I'm not responsible.
jokes.setup: Homer being called in to help with the Lisa/Bart situation
jokes.punchline: Homer's gleeful anticipation of consequence-free entertainment
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer character comedy - his childish delight in others' misfortune when he's not at fault

jokes.text: I put the body in the car, I dump the car in the lake, I put a James Taylor CD in the stereo so they think it's a suicide...
jokes.setup: Homer considering how to deal with Patty being angry
jokes.punchline: Elaborate murder plot ending with oddly specific musical staging detail
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy escalation with perfect absurd detail - James Taylor = suicide is a brilliant specific cultural reference

jokes.text: I'll just keep cutting till I hit something solid.
jokes.setup: Homer agreeing to cut paint out of hair
jokes.punchline: Ominous implication he'll cut through hair, scalp, skull...
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Homer's incompetence - the casual way he describes potentially lethal haircutting

jokes.text: Wake-up-and-look-at-your-hair!
jokes.setup: Homer saying he'll break news gently
jokes.punchline: Immediately shouting bluntly at sleeping Patty
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/punchline with perfect Homer character work - his idea of 'gentle' is hilariously brutal

jokes.text: I didn't do diddily, and certainly not squat.
jokes.punchline: Ned's characteristic wholesome language with diddily/squat contrast
jokes.explanation: Standard Ned Flanders character bit - his quirky speech patterns are recognizable but not particularly clever here

jokes.text: You look like Posh Spice. / That's just what I was going for. / I was going for Scary.
jokes.setup: Compliment about looking like Posh Spice
jokes.punchline: Twin sisters had opposite Spice Girls goals
jokes.explanation: Solid character comedy playing on Patty/Selma's different personalities through Spice Girls references

jokes.text: With your fat fingers and your brain the size of a Superball?
jokes.setup: Disbelief that Homer could do good hair styling
jokes.punchline: Vivid insults about Homer's physical and mental limitations
jokes.explanation: Good insult comedy with specific imagery - 'Superball' is a particularly vivid size comparison for a brain

jokes.text: I meant my hair. / Oh! Oh... / Idiot.
jokes.punchline: Awkward clarification and Homer's embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Predictable sexual misunderstanding joke - functional but relies on obvious double entendre

jokes.text: I put a roadblock on the curly slide.
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual description of dangerous prank
jokes.explanation: Standard Bart mischief - character appropriate but not particularly clever or surprising

jokes.text: There are no more great days, Bart. Just days.
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's existentially depressed response
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Milhouse's sudden philosophical despair delivered with perfect economy and unexpectedness

jokes.text: Did you know it has a chapter before chapter two? A chapter where we meet Nemo's mother.
jokes.punchline: Ominous description of meeting character who dies
jokes.explanation: Building the dark comedy about Disney trauma - good structure leading to the mortality revelation

jokes.text: Not after Chapter One, he doesn't.
jokes.setup: Bart saying Nemo doesn't have a mother
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's matter-of-fact confirmation of her death
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor delivery - Milhouse's deadpan acknowledgment of Disney death with perfect timing

jokes.text: If death can happen to a fish, it can happen to anyone.
jokes.punchline: Flawed logic about fish mortality applying to humans
jokes.explanation: Brilliant child logic - Milhouse's existential breakthrough based on completely backwards reasoning about mortality

jokes.text: You see, in the past, I've been too subtle. It's my curse-- subtlety!
jokes.setup: Milhouse claiming he's been too subtle with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Most obvious kid in school thinks he's too subtle
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Milhouse's complete lack of self-awareness about his obvious crush delivered with perfect confidence

jokes.text: You two look good. Open-casket good.
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment comparing them to corpses
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - starts as compliment, ends as morbid insult. Perfect Moe character voice with excellent economy

jokes.text: Lady, I'm not a hairdresser. I just put a new lid on a couple of trashcans.
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude metaphor reducing haircuts to trash management
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character work - his crude, reductive view of his hairdressing 'skills' fits his personality perfectly

jokes.text: Marge, how much is that in smackaroos? / One hundred.
jokes.punchline: Made-up currency equals exactly the same amount
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay joke - Homer's made-up money term and Marge's deadpan literal response

jokes.text: Hello, airport piano bar.
jokes.punchline: Specific, oddly glamorous comparison to airport piano bar aesthetic
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific and evocative image - airport piano bar as a hairstyle goal is memorably absurd

jokes.text: Well, I open a lot of crazy things when I'm drunk, but I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Asked if he has a salon
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual admission of drunk business ventures
jokes.explanation: Good character humor - Homer's matter-of-fact admission about his drunk business decisions

jokes.text: Just call me 'Homer Fingerhands.'
jokes.punchline: Edward Scissorhands reference with awkward modification
jokes.explanation: Obvious pop culture reference with awkward execution - connects to episode title but lacks originality

jokes.text: It's like a beloved dog that died on your head.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison of hair loss to dead pet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist imagery - Homer's bizarre but oddly touching metaphor for hair loss is memorably weird

jokes.text: Uh, uh, no, I'm, uh... just exploring my body.
jokes.setup: Marge catching Homer brushing his hair
jokes.punchline: Homer's awkward euphemistic excuse
jokes.explanation: Mildly awkward sexual innuendo as Homer tries to avoid admitting vanity about hair

jokes.text: Oh, hair. I'll love you when you're old and gray.
jokes.setup: Homer's private moment with his hair
jokes.punchline: Romantic pledge to hair that's already mostly gray
jokes.explanation: Ironic romantic declaration to hair that's already old and gray - good character moment

jokes.text: I dream of cutting hair, styling hair, making hair shorter in exchange for money.
jokes.punchline: Overly clinical description of basic hairdressing
jokes.explanation: Homer's mechanical description of hairdressing as pure commerce - good character voice

jokes.text: I really thought people would like to harvest their own honey. Oh, how they hated it.
jokes.punchline: Completely misguided business concept and understated failure
jokes.explanation: Good absurd business failure with understated delivery - people harvesting their own honey is hilariously impractical

jokes.text: Lisa Simpson is the smartest, sweetest, most perfectest girl in the whole world
jokes.punchline: Grammar error in compliment about intelligence
jokes.explanation: Ironic grammar mistake while praising intelligence - good character moment for Milhouse's earnestness

jokes.text: Oh yeah, it's a theremin.
jokes.setup: Milhouse about to perform a love song
jokes.punchline: Using an obscure electronic instrument for romance
jokes.explanation: Good nerdy character choice - theremin is perfectly odd instrument for Milhouse's romantic gesture

jokes.text: ♪ Teacher said, don't eat the paste / 'Tis apt to make you spew / I ate the paste, and liked the taste / Passed out and dreamed of you. ♪
jokes.punchline: Love song about eating paste and getting sick
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd romantic song - combining paste-eating with love poetry is perfectly weird Milhouse behavior

jokes.text: Okay-- first of all, it's never wise to use the word 'spew' in a love song.
jokes.punchline: Practical romantic advice about word choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa response - intellectual and practical critique of romance delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: I'm sorry, but I don't love you, Milhouse, and I never will.
jokes.setup: Milhouse asking if love is requited
jokes.punchline: Lisa's brutally direct and final rejection
jokes.explanation: Harsh but honest rejection delivered with perfect Lisa directness - cringe comedy from the finality

jokes.text: Yeah. It was romantic and it rhymed.
jokes.punchline: Extremely low standards for poetry
jokes.explanation: Basic rhyming being enough to impress someone - sets up Taffy as easily pleased character

jokes.text: Well, I used a rhyming dictionary, but it only gives you options. The job of the poet is to say, 'This one, I guess.'
jokes.punchline: Reducing poetry to arbitrary word selection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deconstruction of the creative process - Milhouse's casual dismissal of artistic choice is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Hm! That theremin has paid for itself again and again.
jokes.punchline: Principal measuring romance in terms of equipment ROI
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner character moment - viewing romantic connections through bureaucratic cost-benefit analysis

jokes.text: All day long, Apu goes on about Lady Gaga, who is doing nothing that Rava Khatan was not doing 20 years ago, while she was a member of Parliament.
jokes.punchline: Obscure comparison between Lady Gaga and fictional politician
jokes.explanation: Absurdist cultural criticism - combining pop music with made-up political figures is memorably weird

jokes.text: Funny how my Timothy always uses the short-form baptism when there's a basketball game he wants to watch...
jokes.punchline: Clergy cutting corners for sports
jokes.explanation: Mild religious humor about practical compromises - typical salon gossip elevated slightly

jokes.text: All day long I have to listen to women talk. Not a good time!
jokes.punchline: Homer's sexist complaint about his job requirement
jokes.explanation: Basic Homer sexism - fits character but relies on lazy gender stereotypes

jokes.text: Well, I've worn a lot of different braces in my time.
jokes.punchline: Medical equipment as qualification for expertise
jokes.explanation: Good Milhouse character detail - his medical problems as accidental expertise

jokes.text: This morning I got to second base... on our walk around the softball diamond.
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of baseball metaphor
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - sexual innuendo becomes innocent literal baseball reference. Good Milhouse naivety

jokes.text: I dunno. It's one of those mysteries, like how do my clothes get clean and put back in my drawers?
jokes.punchline: Comparing romance to basic domestic ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good child character moment - Milhouse's complete ignorance of domestic life as metaphor

jokes.text: I know Kirk resents that the dog likes me more, but Rex is just establishing dominance over the weaker male.
jokes.punchline: Applying animal behavior to human relationships
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about pet psychology applied to marriage - typical salon oversharing

jokes.text: Hey, I don't want a conversation. Oh?! I just want you to listen while I talk about the loser I'm married to.
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting herself with one-sided talking
jokes.explanation: Good observation about salon dynamics - the contradiction between wanting no conversation but talking anyway

jokes.text: When I look at you, all I see are the foibles that drive your women crazy! Foibles! Foibles! Foibles!
jokes.setup: Homer's psychological breakdown from listening to complaints
jokes.punchline: Manic repetition of 'foibles' while seeing customer flaws
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's breakdown - the repetition of 'foibles' shows his psychological overload

jokes.text: I can also make very annoying noises.
jokes.punchline: Threatening with annoying sounds instead of police power
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - treating annoying noises as legitimate police intimidation

jokes.text: I'll teach you how to make love to your wife! / What? No! Just cut her hair!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to escape hair cutting demand
jokes.punchline: Sexual misunderstanding and panicked clarification
jokes.explanation: Predictable sexual misunderstanding - functional but relies on obvious double entendre

jokes.text: Why don't you do here what you do at the nuclear plant-- namely, suck?
jokes.setup: Homer asking for help to get out of hairdressing
jokes.punchline: Lenny's blunt assessment of Homer's work performance
jokes.explanation: Solid friend humor - Lenny's direct acknowledgment of Homer's professional incompetence as solution

jokes.text: So, I told Timothy you cannot keep basing sermons on old Seinfelds.
jokes.punchline: Clergy using sitcom episodes as sermon material
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about modern clergy adapting pop culture for religious purposes

jokes.text: Like Mozart and Johnny Knoxville, my genius cannot be stopped!
jokes.setup: Homer having a realization about sucking on purpose
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to Mozart and Johnny Knoxville as geniuses
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist pairing - Mozart and Johnny Knoxville as equivalent geniuses is perfectly random and memorable

jokes.text: I'm committing Barbicide!
jokes.punchline: Pun on barbicide (hair tool disinfectant) and suicide
jokes.explanation: Clever industry-specific pun combining barbershop tool with dramatic declaration

jokes.text: Oh, why doesn't anything kill me?
jokes.punchline: Homer's frustrated complaint about his indestructibility
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer invincibility joke - his casual frustration with surviving everything

jokes.text: I hear he goes to California on his vacations. / That's the first thing everyone knows about Kyle LoBianco!
jokes.punchline: His vacation destination as his defining characteristic
jokes.explanation: Good satire of elementary school social hierarchies - vacation destination as primary identity marker

jokes.text: Marge, do you hear it? Listen! It's the hair... growing! Always growing! Blonde, brunette, auburn!
jokes.punchline: Paranoid delusions about hearing hair growth and color
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's hairdresser trauma - specific hair colors add to the paranoid detail

jokes.text: We're just trying to put our relationship back together. In the sack, he's Salisbury steak, everywhere else, creamed corn.
jokes.setup: Taffy explaining her relationship with Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Food metaphors for sexual performance from a 10-year-old
jokes.explanation: Shocking adult content from child character - the food metaphors are specific and memorable, creating major tonal dissonance

jokes.text: How miserable do I have to be before you're happy?
jokes.setup: Milhouse's frustration with Lisa's mixed signals
jokes.punchline: Direct question about relationship dynamics
jokes.explanation: Sharp relationship observation from Milhouse - cuts to the core of the Lisa dynamic with good economy

jokes.text: Everything's comin' up Milhouse!
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Milhouse catchphrase at perfect moment - high character integration and quotability

jokes.text: My girlfriend isn't feeling well tonight. / Krusty the Clown and Disco Stu's girlfriend!
jokes.setup: Disco Stu arriving alone at event
jokes.punchline: Announcer pairing him with Krusty who also came alone
jokes.explanation: Good social awkwardness humor - announcer's solution to two dateless men

jokes.text: Oh, yes, ladies, I've outdone myself. Outdone myself by not doing it at all!
jokes.punchline: Admitting he didn't do it while still claiming credit
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - taking credit for not doing something while revealing the deception

jokes.text: No, no, no, no, no, no, no, Julio is my little secret! / I've said too much!
jokes.setup: Marge trying to keep her hairdresser secret
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing the name after claiming secrecy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Marge's inability to keep secrets under pressure

jokes.text: That's right, ladies. And I never heard a complaint I didn't sympathetically cluck to.
jokes.setup: Julio being revealed as the real hairdresser
jokes.punchline: Perfect description of salon customer service
jokes.explanation: Perfect salon stereotype with specific language - 'sympathetically cluck' captures the professional empathy perfectly

jokes.text: Okay, first five customers get a discount. Next five hear secrets about the first five.
jokes.punchline: Salon gossip as explicit business model
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deconstruction of salon culture - making the gossip economy explicit and transactional

jokes.text: As if anyone but you could make me feel this beautiful.
jokes.punchline: Crediting Homer for beauty he didn't create
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment - Marge's love overriding the facts about who did her hair

jokes.text: Aw, sweetie, thanks for saving me from the horrible life Warren Beatty lived in Shampoo.
jokes.setup: Thanking Homer for getting out of hairdressing
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to Warren Beatty's hairdresser character
jokes.explanation: Cultural reference to 1970s film - somewhat obscure but fits Marge's character knowledge

jokes.text: As long as it doesn't involve losing weight or changing my pants.
jokes.punchline: Setting very specific lazy limitations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - his romantic gesture with hilariously specific lazy limitations

tmdb_overview: Homer opens his own hair salon after discovering he is a talented stylist; Milhouse professes his love for Lisa.
